Data-level parallelism essentially is I have one instruction that says, oh, take sort of an array of data and apply the same operation on every element of the array.

So all of these elements of an array are processed in parallel. That's what the vector instruction does. Then we also have instruction-level parallelism. Every single core that you have on your laptop, on the desktop, when you log into the machines in the department, on your phone even, has an out-of-order superscalar processor, which means that even though every core has a sequential stream of instructions, it can execute them multiple at a time and out of order to exploit some parallelism that exists between instructions.
